[
  {
    "idea": "Weekly surge in Google Trends search for 'job quitting' signals labor market stress",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search interest for 'job quitting'",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "A sudden rise in searches for quitting jobs suggests rising labor market stress and potential wage inflation.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Consumer discretionary stocks face margin pressure from rising labor costs."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly search interest increases by 20% week-over-week",
      "exit": "After 3 weeks or if interest declines by 10% week-over-week",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 18,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Labor unrest and quitting are common and often spike seasonally or with economic changes."
  },
